summary: Co-hosts under Jesse Marsch — energetic, direct, and built on pressing and counter-pressing. The fastest team in the 2024 Copa América at moving the ball forward, hunting their first-ever World Cup point on home soil (third appearance after 1986 and 2022).
style: 4-4-2 — win the ball and attack quickly in transition, progressing through wide areas to the wingers (Buchanan, Ali Ahmed, Davies), with the left side supported by an advancing full-back (Davies or Richie Laryea). Jonathan David partners Oluwaseyi or Larin and drops in to link play. High, lane-blocking press out of the 4-4-2.

counterReliance.note: Direct, transition-first — fastest direct speed at Copa América 2024

strengths: Fastest team at Copa América 2024 (direct speed 1.9 m/s), only 2.8 passes per sequence — purely direct
Energetic, organised high press — block lanes, force wide, then go for the ball
Jonathan David (Juventus) as the link forward; pace out wide
weaknesses: Alphonso Davies' fitness in serious doubt (ACL Mar 2025, then two further muscle injuries)
Few sustained-possession solutions — reliant on transition and wide service

keyPlayers.name: Alphonso Davies
keyPlayers.note: Pace and dribbling down the left — but injury-hit (ACL + hamstring + muscle); scored Canada's first men's WC goal in 2022

keyPlayers.name: Tajon Buchanan
keyPlayers.note: If Davies isn't fit, the man to watch — pace, dribbling, a counter threat

keyPlayers.name: Jonathan David
keyPlayers.note: Drops to link play and attack the space behind the opposition midfield; essential to the attack

tactics: Press from a 4-4-2 — block passing lanes, shepherd play wide, then spring the trap — and break at speed once the ball is won. Maxime Crepeau got the nod in goal over Dayne St. Clair days before the tournament. The high press versus Switzerland's fluid build-up could decide the group.
notes: Still chasing their first-ever World Cup point (lost all three in both 1986 and 2022).
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group B (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
